author: dojo-community
name: query
context: Extract fields, filter arrays, and emit script-friendly values from JSON streams or files with jq
info: This skill is for the most common jq job: turning bulky JSON from APIs, CLIs, logs, or config files into the small set of values an agent needs for the next step. It covers field access, array traversal, `select`, `map`, fallbacks, variable binding, raw output for shell use, and exit-status checks for control flow. The packaged script wraps jq with predictable environment-variable inputs so agents can run extraction and filtering tasks without reassembling flag logic each time.
triggers: print one value from package json
check a json condition in bash
turn json into plain text with jq
body:
# jq Query

Load this skill when the job is to look up values, narrow results, or branch on JSON without mutating the original structure.

## Common Patterns

~~~bash
jq ".id" issue.json
jq -r ".version" package.json
jq ".items[] | select(.state == \"open\") | {id, title}" issues.json
jq ".deployments | map(select(.env == \"prod\")) | length" deployments.json
jq -e ".status == \"ok\"" health.json > /dev/null
~~~

## API and CLI Pipelines

jq is often chained after a JSON-producing command. Use `-r` when the next shell command needs raw text instead of JSON strings.

~~~bash
curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/jqlang/jq | jq -r ".clone_url"
gh api repos/jqlang/jq/issues | jq ".[] | select(.state == \"open\") | .title"
terraform output -json | jq -r ".bucket_name.value"
~~~

## Variables and Fallbacks

Use `--arg` or `--argjson` when the filter depends on caller input. Use the alternative operator `//` when a missing or null value should fall back to something safer.

~~~bash
jq --arg env prod ".deployments[] | select(.environment == $env) | .url" deploys.json
jq -r ".metadata.owner // \"unknown\"" app.json
~~~

## Shell-Friendly Checks

The `-e` flag turns jq into a predicate engine for Bash conditionals.

~~~bash
if jq -e ".ready == true" status.json > /dev/null; then
  echo "ready"
else
  echo "not ready"
fi
~~~

For JSON rewrites, continue to [[jq/transform]]. For large payloads, continue to [[jq/streaming]].

sections.title: Field and element access
sections.body: Use `.field`, `.[index]`, `.items[]`, and object literals such as `{id, url}` to pull only the fields that matter. These are the smallest useful jq queries and are often enough for follow-up shell interpolation or logging.

sections.title: Filtering arrays and objects
sections.body: Use `select(...)` inside a stream or `map(select(...))` when the result should remain an array. Combine field access, comparisons, and pipes to narrow large responses into a short actionable list.

sections.title: Script-friendly output
sections.body: Use `-r` when Bash or another command needs plain text, `-c` when one compact JSON value per line is easier to consume, and `-e` when jq should drive conditional logic through its exit code. These three flags are the difference between a human-friendly jq command and an automation-friendly jq command.

sections.title: Caller-provided variables
sections.body: Use `--arg` for strings and `--argjson` for already-encoded JSON values. This lets the caller parameterize a filter without unsafe string interpolation in the jq program itself. See [[jq/cli#args-and-vars]] for the full argument surface.

links.context: Quoting, raw output, and exit status flags used by query workflows
Move from extraction to mutation and file rewrites
Switch to streaming when payloads are large
Compose jq with other shell tools

related.note: Correct invocation flags matter as much as the filter itself
HTTP responses are a primary source of jq input
Once you need to rewrite structure instead of read it, switch to transform workflows

scripts.name: Run Query
scripts.description: Execute a jq extraction or filtering command against stdin, a file, or inline JSON

JQ_FILE.description: Path to the input JSON file; omit to read stdin or JQ_INPUT

JQ_INPUT.description: Inline JSON or raw text input

JQ_NULL_INPUT.description: Use -n and do not read stdin, JQ_FILE, or JQ_INPUT

JQ_RAW_INPUT.description: Treat input as raw text lines via -R

JQ_SLURP.description: Read all inputs into one array or one raw string via -s

JQ_RAW_OUTPUT.description: Write strings without JSON quoting via -r

JQ_VARS_JSON.description: JSON object whose keys become jq variables through --argjson

input.description: Inline JSON or raw text input

variables.description: Named values exposed to jq as variables

stdout.description: jq output written to standard output

depends.reason: Query workflows depend on correct invocation flags and variable passing
The packaged script runs in Bash and is commonly embedded in shell pipelines
